curriculum.subject: Chemistry
curriculum.overallDescription: 9 units, 9 chapters, and 11 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, uncertainty, laws of chemical combination, Dalton's atomic theory
Calculate atomic/molecular masses, mole concept, molar mass, percentage composition, stoichiometry
Trace discoveries of sub-atomic particles and development leading to Bohr's model for Hydrogen atom
Apply modern periodic law and interpret the present form of Periodic Table
Analyze bond parameters and predict molecular geometry using VSEPR theory
Relate Equilibrium Constant K, Reaction Quotient Q, and Gibbs Energy G
Analyze redox reactions in terms of electrode processes
Apply fundamental concepts in Organic Reaction Mechanism
Differentiate between various classes of hydrocarbons

topics: Mole Concept, Molar Masses, Percentage Composition
Discovery and Models of the Atom
Sub-atomic Particles, Bohr's Model for Hydrogen

practicals.title: Cutting glass tube and glass rod
Bending a glass tube
Drawing out a glass jet
Boring a cork
Determination of melting point of an organic compound
practicals.details: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

practicals.title: Determination of boiling point of an organic compound
practicals.details: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

practicals.title: Crystallization of Alum, Copper Sulphate, or Benzoic Acid
practicals.details: Purification of impure sample (Characterization and Purification)

practicals.title: Determination of pH using fruit juices/acid/base/salt solutions
Comparing the pH of strong and weak acids of same concentration
Study pH change in titration using universal indicator
Study pH change by common-ion effect (weak acids/bases)
Volumetric Analysis
Salt Analysis
Content Based Experiment
Project Work
Class record and viva
Study shift in equilibrium: Ferric ions/thiocyanate ions
Study shift in equilibrium: [Co(H2O)6]2+ and chloride ions
Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid/Sodium carbonate
Determination of strength of NaOH by titration against Oxalic acid
Determination of strength of HCl by titration against Sodium Carbonate
Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt
Preparation of lyophilic sol (starch, egg albumin, gum)
Preparation of lyophobic sol (aluminum hydroxide, ferric hydroxide, arsenous sulphide)
Dialysis of prepared sols
Study role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing emulsion
Effect of concentration and temperature on rate (Sodium Thiosulphate + HCl)
Study reaction rates: Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide
Clock Reaction: Potassium Iodate + Sodium Sulphate (starch indicator)
Enthalpy of dissolution (Copper Sulphate or Potassium Nitrate)
Enthalpy of neutralization (HCl + NaOH)
Determination of enthalpy change: Acetone and Chloroform interaction
Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with concentration change
Separation of pigments from leaves/flowers by paper chromatography (Rf values)
Preparation of double salt: Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum
Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate
Preparation of Organic Compounds (Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, Aniline yellow/2-Naphthol dye)
Tests for functional groups (Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic, amino)
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples/foodstuffs
Determination of concentration/molarity of KMnO4 solution (titration against Oxalic acid or Mohr's salt)
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XI Practical: Total Marks - 30, Time - 2 Hours
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

internal_assessment.scheme_name: Practical Examination for Visually Challenged Learners Classes XI and XII
components: Written test (based on given/prescribed practical's): 10 Marks

duration_details.question_paper_format: Legibly typed, total of 15 practical skill based very short answer type questions. Student required to answer any 10.
duration_details.practical_file_requirement: Record at least five of the listed experiments as per specific instructions.

internal_assessment.student_type: General Students - Written Test for Practical
components: Total of 15 practical skill based very short answer type questions.
Student required to answer any 10 questions.

experiments: Cutting glass tube and glass rod
Bending a glass tube
Drawing out a glass jet
Boring a cork

sections.section_name: B. Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
experiments: Determination of melting point of an organic compound.
Determination of boiling point of an organic compound.
Crystallization of impure sample (Alum, Copper Sulphate, Benzoic Acid).
Determination of pH of some solutions obtained from fruit juices, solution of known and varied concentrations of acids, bases and salts using pH paper or universal indicator.
Comparing the pH of solutions of strong and weak acids of same concentration.
Study the pH change in the titration of a strong base using a universal indicator.
Study the pH change by common-ion in case of weak acids and weak bases.
Study the shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions.
Study the shift in equilibrium between [Co(H 2 O) 6 ] 2+ and chloride ions.
Using a mechanical balance/electronic balance.
Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid.
Determination of strength of a given solution of Sodium hydroxide by titrating it against standard solution of Oxalic acid.
Preparation of standard solution of Sodium carbonate.
Determination of strength of a given solution of hydrochloric acid by titrating it against standard Sodium Carbonate solution.

Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: Pb2+, Cu2+, Al3+, Fe3+, Mn2+, Ni2+, Zn2+, Co2+, Ca2+, Sr2+, Ba2+, Mg2+, NH4+; Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, NO3 -, NO2 -, Cl-, Br-, I-, SO4 2-, PO4 3-, CH3COO-).
Detection of -Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds.

visually_challenged_experiments.section_name: A. Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
experiments: Crystallization of an impure sample (copper sulphate, benzoic acid).
Determination of pH of some solutions obtained from fruit juices...
Comparing the pH of solutions of strong and weak acids...
Study the shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions.
Study the shift in equilibrium between [Co(H 2 O) 6 ] 2+ and chloride ions.
Preparation of standard solution of oxalic acid.
Determination of molarity of a given solution of sodium hydroxide...
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: NH4+; Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, Cl-, CH3COO-).
Detection of Nitrogen in the given organic compound.
Detection of Halogen in the given organic compound.

assessment_components: Identification/familiarity with the apparatus (Beaker, tripod stand, wire gauze, glass rod, funnel, filter paper, Bunsen burner, test tube, etc.)

experiments: Preparation of one lyophilic and one lyophobic sol (Lyophilic: starch, egg albumin, gum; Lyophobic: aluminum hydroxide, ferric hydroxide, arsenous sulphide).
Dialysis of sol-prepared in (a) above.
Study of the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing the emulsion.
Effect of concentration and temperature on the rate of reaction between Sodium Thiosulphate and Hydrochloric acid.
Study of reaction rates (Reaction of Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide; Reaction between Potassium Iodate, KIO3 and Sodium Sulphate).
Enthalpy of dissolution of Copper Sulphate or Potassium Nitrate.
Enthalpy of neutralization of strong acid (HCI) and strong base (NaOH).
Determination of enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform.
Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn 2+ || Cu 2+ /Cu with change in concentration of electrolytes (CuSO4 or ZnSO4).
Separation of pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers by paper chromatography.
Separation of constituents present in an inorganic mixture containing two cations only.
Preparation of double salt (Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum).
Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate.
Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, Aniline yellow or 2 - Naphthol Aniline dye.

sections.section_name: H. Tests for the functional groups present in organic compounds
experiments: Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino (Primary) groups.

sections.section_name: I. Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples
experiments: Detection in given foodstuffs.

sections.section_name: J. Determination of concentration/ molarity of KMnO4 solution by titrating it against a standard solution of: Oxalic acid, Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate

experiments: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: Pb2+, Cu2+, Al3+, Fe3+, Mn2+, Ni2+ Zn2+ Co2+ Ca2+ Sr2+ Ba2+ Mg2+, NH4 +; Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, NO3 -, NO2 -, Cl-, Br-, I-, SO4 2-, PO4 3-, CH3COO-, C2 O4 2-).
Preparation of one lyophilic and one lyophobic sol (Lyophilic: starch, egg albumin; Lyophobic: Ferric hydroxide).
Separation of pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers by paper chromatography.

visually_challenged_experiments.section_name: C. Tests for the functional groups present in organic compounds
experiments: Alcoholic and Carboxylic groups.
Aldehyde and Kenotic groups.

visually_challenged_experiments.section_name: D. Characteristic tests of carbohydrates and proteins in the given foodstuffs

experiments: Potash Alum.

assessment_components: Identification/familiarity with the apparatus (Beaker, glass rod, tripod stand, wire gauze, Bunsen burner, Whatman filter paper, gas jar, capillary tube, pestle and mortar, test tubes, tongs, burette, pipette, conical flask, standard flask, clamp stand, funnel).
Hands-on Assessment: Identification/familiarity with the apparatus.

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources. Any other investigatory project, which involves about 10 periods of work, can be chosen with the approval of the teacher.
suggested_projects: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of the methods of purification of water

Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., depending upon the regional variation in drinking water and study of causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit (if any).
Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves.
Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers.
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices.

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources. Any other investigatory project, which involves about 10 periods of work, can be chosen with the approval of the teacher.
suggested_projects: Study of the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening.
Study of quantity of casein present in different samples of milk.
Preparation of soybean milk and its comparison with the natural milk with respect to curd formation, effect of temperature, etc.
Study of the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions (temperature, concentration, time, etc.)
Study of digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature on it.
Comparative study of the rate of fermentation of following materials: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice, etc.
Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom).
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric power, chili powder and pepper.

components: Total of 15 practical skill based very short answer type questions.
Student required to answer any 10 questions.

experiments: Cutting glass tube and glass rod
Bending a glass tube
Drawing out a glass jet
Boring a cork

sections.section_name: B. Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
experiments: Determination of melting point of an organic compound.
Determination of boiling point of an organic compound.
Crystallization of impure sample (Alum, Copper Sulphate, Benzoic Acid).
Determination of pH of some solutions obtained from fruit juices, solution of known and varied concentrations of acids, bases and salts using pH paper or universal indicator.
Comparing the pH of solutions of strong and weak acids of same concentration.
Study the pH change in the titration of a strong base using a universal indicator.
Study the pH change by common-ion in case of weak acids and weak bases.
Study the shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions.
Study the shift in equilibrium between [Co(H 2 O) 6 ] 2+ and chloride ions.
Using a mechanical balance/electronic balance.
Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid.
Determination of strength of a given solution of Sodium hydroxide by titrating it against standard solution of Oxalic acid.
Preparation of standard solution of Sodium carbonate.
Determination of strength of a given solution of hydrochloric acid by titrating it against standard Sodium Carbonate solution.

Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: Pb2+, Cu2+, Al3+, Fe3+, Mn2+, Ni2+, Zn2+, Co2+, Ca2+, Sr2+, Ba2+, Mg2+, NH4+; Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, NO3 -, NO2 -, Cl-, Br-, I-, SO4 2-, PO4 3-, CH3COO-).
Detection of -Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds.

visually_challenged_experiments.section_name: A. Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
experiments: Crystallization of an impure sample (copper sulphate, benzoic acid).
Determination of pH of some solutions obtained from fruit juices...
Comparing the pH of solutions of strong and weak acids...
Study the shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions.
Study the shift in equilibrium between [Co(H 2 O) 6 ] 2+ and chloride ions.
Preparation of standard solution of oxalic acid.
Determination of molarity of a given solution of sodium hydroxide...
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: NH4+; Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, Cl-, CH3COO-).
Detection of Nitrogen in the given organic compound.
Detection of Halogen in the given organic compound.

assessment_components: Identification/familiarity with the apparatus (Beaker, tripod stand, wire gauze, glass rod, funnel, filter paper, Bunsen burner, test tube, etc.)

experiments: Preparation of one lyophilic and one lyophobic sol (Lyophilic: starch, egg albumin, gum; Lyophobic: aluminum hydroxide, ferric hydroxide, arsenous sulphide).
Dialysis of sol-prepared in (a) above.
Study of the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing the emulsion.
Effect of concentration and temperature on the rate of reaction between Sodium Thiosulphate and Hydrochloric acid.
Study of reaction rates (Reaction of Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide; Reaction between Potassium Iodate, KIO3 and Sodium Sulphate).
Enthalpy of dissolution of Copper Sulphate or Potassium Nitrate.
Enthalpy of neutralization of strong acid (HCI) and strong base (NaOH).
Determination of enthalpy change during interaction between Acetone and Chloroform.
Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn 2+ || Cu 2+ /Cu with change in concentration of electrolytes (CuSO4 or ZnSO4).
Separation of pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers by paper chromatography.
Separation of constituents present in an inorganic mixture containing two cations only.
Preparation of double salt (Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum).
Preparation of Potassium Ferric Oxalate.
Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, p-Nitroacetanilide, Aniline yellow or 2 - Naphthol Aniline dye.

sections.section_name: H. Tests for the functional groups present in organic compounds
experiments: Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic and amino (Primary) groups.

sections.section_name: I. Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples
experiments: Detection in given foodstuffs.

sections.section_name: J. Determination of concentration/ molarity of KMnO4 solution by titrating it against a standard solution of: Oxalic acid, Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate

experiments: Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (Cations: Pb2+, Cu2+, Al3+, Fe3+, Mn2+, Ni2+ Zn2+ Co2+ Ca2+ Sr2+ Ba2+ Mg2+, NH4 +; Anions: CO3 2-, S2-, SO3 2-, NO3 -, NO2 -, Cl-, Br-, I-, SO4 2-, PO4 3-, CH3COO-, C2 O4 2-).
Preparation of one lyophilic and one lyophobic sol (Lyophilic: starch, egg albumin; Lyophobic: Ferric hydroxide).
Separation of pigments from extracts of leaves and flowers by paper chromatography.

visually_challenged_experiments.section_name: C. Tests for the functional groups present in organic compounds
experiments: Alcoholic and Carboxylic groups.
Aldehyde and Kenotic groups.

visually_challenged_experiments.section_name: D. Characteristic tests of carbohydrates and proteins in the given foodstuffs

experiments: Potash Alum.

assessment_components: Identification/familiarity with the apparatus (Beaker, glass rod, tripod stand, wire gauze, Bunsen burner, Whatman filter paper, gas jar, capillary tube, pestle and mortar, test tubes, tongs, burette, pipette, conical flask, standard flask, clamp stand, funnel).
Hands-on Assessment: Identification/familiarity with the apparatus.

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources. Any other investigatory project, which involves about 10 periods of work, can be chosen with the approval of the teacher.
suggested_projects: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
Study of the methods of purification of water

Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride, etc., depending upon the regional variation in drinking water and study of causes of presence of these ions above permissible limit (if any).
Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and the effect of addition of Sodium carbonate on it
Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves.
Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers.
Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices.

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources. Any other investigatory project, which involves about 10 periods of work, can be chosen with the approval of the teacher.
suggested_projects: Study of the presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening.
Study of quantity of casein present in different samples of milk.
Preparation of soybean milk and its comparison with the natural milk with respect to curd formation, effect of temperature, etc.
Study of the effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions (temperature, concentration, time, etc.)
Study of digestion of starch by salivary amylase and effect of pH and temperature on it.
Comparative study of the rate of fermentation of following materials: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice, etc.
Extraction of essential oils present in Saunf (aniseed), Ajwain (carom), Illaichi (cardamom).
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric power, chili powder and pepper.

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Conceptual Foundations and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Equip learners with tools to understand the working of Chemistry, develop conceptual foundations, represent phenomena at macroscopic/molecular/symbolic levels, identify patterns, and apply knowledge to real-life situations.
competencies: Understand nature of matter, properties, measurement, uncertainty, laws of chemical combination, Dalton's atomic theory
Calculate atomic/molecular masses, mole concept, molar mass, percentage composition, stoichiometry

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Models
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to comprehend the evolution from sub-atomic particles discovery through Bohr's model to the quantum mechanical model.
competencies: Trace discoveries of sub-atomic particles and development leading to Bohr's model for Hydrogen atom

competency_groups.title: Periodic Classification and Trends
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify elements, understand the periodic law, electronic configurations, block classification (s-, p-, d-, f-), and analyze trends in properties.
competencies: Apply modern periodic law and interpret the present form of Periodic Table
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding Theories
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply various bonding theories (Kossel-Lewis, VSEPR, Valence Bond, Molecular Orbital) and understand hybridization and hydrogen bonding.
competencies: Analyze bond parameters and predict molecular geometry using VSEPR theory

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics and Spontaneity
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to measure enthalpy changes, understand spontaneity, Gibbs energy change, and equilibrium conditions.
competencies: Calculate ∆U and ∆H using calorimetry

competency_groups.title: Equilibrium Principles
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to analyze dynamic equilibrium in physical and chemical processes, apply the law of chemical equilibrium, and understand factors affecting equilibria including ionic equilibrium.
competencies: Relate Equilibrium Constant K, Reaction Quotient Q, and Gibbs Energy G

competency_groups.title: Redox Processes
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to identify oxidation-reduction reactions through classical ideas, electron transfer, and oxidation numbers.
competencies: Analyze redox reactions in terms of electrode processes

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals and Mechanisms
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to represent organic compounds structurally, classify them, apply IUPAC nomenclature, understand isomerism, reaction mechanisms, purification methods, and analysis techniques.
competencies: Apply fundamental concepts in Organic Reaction Mechanism
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify hydrocarbons, understand properties of alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, aromatic compounds, and assess carcinogenicity/toxicity.
competencies: Differentiate between various classes of hydrocarbons

competency_groups.title: Solutions and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to express concentration, understand solubility, vapor pressure of solutions, ideal/non-ideal behavior, and determine molar mass using colligative properties.
competencies: Apply concepts of abnormal molecular masses

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry and Energy Conversion
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand electrochemical cells, Nernst equation, conductance, electrolysis, batteries, fuel cells, and corrosion.
competencies: Analyze galvanic cells and apply the Nernst Equation

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics and Reaction Rates
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to analyze factors influencing reaction rates, integrated rate equations, temperature dependence, and collision theory.
competencies: Interpret the Temperature Dependence of the rate of a reaction

competency_groups.title: d- and f-Block Elements Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand electronic configurations, general properties of transition elements, lanthanoids/actinoids, and applications.
competencies: Correlate position in Periodic Table with electronic configuration

competency_groups.title: Coordination Chemistry and Bonding
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apply Werner's theory, understand nomenclature, isomerism, bonding in coordination compounds (including metal carbonyls), and applications.
competencies: Apply IUPAC nomenclature for Coordination Compounds

competency_groups.title: Organic Halogen Derivatives and Functional Groups
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify haloalkanes/haloarenes, understand preparation methods, physical/chemical properties, and reactions of alcohols, phenols, ethers.
competencies: Analyze the nature of C–X bond in Haloalkanes

competency_groups.title: Carbonyl Compounds Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand nomenclature, preparation, physical properties, chemical reactions, and uses of aldehydes, ketones, and carboxylic acids.
competencies: Differentiate between Aldehydic and Ketonic groups

competency_groups.title: Amines and Diazonium Salts Synthesis
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to classify amines, understand preparation methods, physical/chemical properties, and the importance of diazonium salts in synthesis.
competencies: Apply chemical reactions for synthesis of aromatic compounds using Diazonium Salts

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules and Biochemistry Interface
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand the structure, classification, and functions of carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, vitamins, nucleic acids, and hormones.
competencies: Identify key biomolecules including Enzymes and Hormones

competency_groups.title: Surface Chemistry and Colloids
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to distinguish between true solutions, colloids, and suspensions; understand adsorption (physisorption/chemisorption), lyophilic/lyophobic properties, coagulation, and emulsions.
competencies: Analyze the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing emulsions

competency_groups.title: Isolation and Extraction Principles
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to understand principles of extraction, concentration methods (oxidation/reduction), electrolytic refining, and polymerization concepts.
competencies: Apply general principles for isolation of elements
competency_groups.unit_name: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements
competency_groups.chapter_name: General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements

competency_groups.title: Chemistry in Everyday Life
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to apprise themselves of chemicals used in medicines (analgesics, antibiotics, etc.) and food preservatives/sweeteners.
competencies: Identify applications of antiseptics, disinfectants, and antimicrobials

competency_groups.title: Laboratory Techniques and Safety
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to perform basic laboratory techniques (cutting, bending glass), characterization/purification of substances, pH determination, and qualitative/quantitative analysis safely.
competencies: Execute micro-chemical methods for practical experiments
competency_groups.chapter_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances

competency_groups.title: Scientific Inquiry and Problem Solving
competency_groups.description: Enable learners to contribute to frontier research areas, inculcate problem-solving skills, integrate life skills/values, and conduct scientific investigations/projects.
competencies: Design and execute investigatory projects involving laboratory testing

marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XI Practical: Total Marks - 30, Time - 2 Hours
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

internal_assessment.scheme_name: Practical Examination for Visually Challenged Learners Classes XI and XII
components: Written test (based on given/prescribed practical's): 10 Marks
